trigger: OC2A physically moved to new location/network; post-move infrastructure health diagnostic requested

summary.preRecoveryStatus: degraded (1 critical: PG down; 3 stale markers)
summary.postRecoveryStatus: ok — all checks green, all 4 critical services up, health-state.json: status=ok, issues=[], exitCode=0
summary.verdict: RECOVERABLE — one stuck PostgreSQL recovered via stale-lock cleanup + launchctl kickstart. Rest of stack is healthy and Tailscale/network adapted cleanly. No firewall/Tailscale-auth/SSH-key/OpenClaw-config changes were made.

evidence.launchctl_print: gui/501/homebrew.mxcl.postgresql@16 — runs=1340, last exit code=1, state='spawn scheduled'
evidence.pg_log_recent: 1341 lines of 'FATAL: lock file postmaster.pid already exists — Is another postmaster (PID 688) running in data directory'
evidence.stale_lock_file: /Users/ainchorsoc2a/homebrew/var/postgresql@16/postmaster.pid — last modified 2026-07-14 18:39, references PID 688, but PID 688 is now 'appplaceholdersyncd' (unrelated macOS process)
evidence.root_cause: PostgreSQL died on 2026-07-14 18:39 (date matches mtime of postmaster.pid and last PG boot line in log) but failed to remove its lock file. launchd has been thrashing for ~4 days, restarting every ~10s, every restart failing on the stale lock, never able to bind 5432.

findings.whyItHappened: Crash on 2026-07-14 (date coincident with prior move/transfer activity in the area) left a stale postmaster.pid. KeepAlive=true + ExitTimeOut behaviour: launchd retries indefinitely, but PG itself refuses to start when it sees a lock. The deadlock is between launchd (which has nothing to compare against) and PG (which has a stale lock).
findings.remediation: Removed stale /Users/ainchorsoc2a/homebrew/var/postgresql@16/postmaster.pid; issued launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/homebrew.mxcl.postgresql@16; PG now listening on 127.0.0.1:5432 (post-fix verification in F1.r).

evidence.lan_ip: 192.168.40.51 (NEW — different from prior network; netcheck noted 'gateway and self IP changed: gw=192.168.40.1 self=192.168.40.51')
evidence.peers: 9 peers in tailnet, OC1 (ainchorss-mac-mini) last seen 1d ago (expected — locked for dev/test 2026-07-15), kenmun-x1 active via direct 175.33.104.193:62718

findings.remediation: None required. Tailscale handled the network change transparently.

evidence.other_interfaces: en0, en2-en7, anpi0-2, ap1, utun1-4 all present; en1 carries the active default route

findings.remediation: None required. DHCP from new router worked.

evidence.http_200: curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/health → HTTP 200 in 8.6ms; / → HTTP 200 in 1.4ms

findings.remediation: None required. Gateway is healthy and survived the move.

findings.status: NOT LISTENING — but is this a regression?

evidence.plist_check: Only ai.openclaw.gateway plist exists; no separate 18791 sidecar plist; gateway plist only specifies --port 18789

findings.interpretation: Per TOOLS.md port convention, 18791 is the browser control sidecar. No plist or process was found for it on the post-move system. May be (a) lazily spawned by gateway on first browser-automation call, (b) intentionally not running in this mode, or (c) lost in the move. No agent currently requested a browser action, so the sidecar absence is invisible to the running stack.
findings.remediation: Left as-is. Will need Ken/CREST decision: install browser-sidecar plist + add to launchctl? Or keep 18791 as ephemeral? (See Out-of-Scope section.)

evidence.mc_admin_info: Access Denied — explained: `mc` has no `local` alias configured; the service is up but the CLI client lacks credentials/alias. Not a server issue.

findings.remediation: None required. (Optional: `mc alias set local http://127.0.0.1:9000 ainchors-minio <pwd>` if MC CLI use is needed.)

evidence.show_call: API /api/show returned full license blob — service fully functional

findings.interpretation: Model name 'gemma4' matches cost-state.json (which references gemma4:e2b, gemma4:26b, gemma4:31b-cloud) — this is the canonical local model name. Not a regression.

evidence.note: Subsequent 03:00 AEST runs (2026-07-17, 2026-07-18) are pending their next scheduled window — not missed. cron daemon is alive and well.

findings.remediation: None required. Cron is healthy and will run on its next slot.

evidence.session-store-cleanup.cron.log: clean — all agent=N summaries show failed_agents=none, DONE moved=0
evidence.stability_dir: /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/logs/stability/ — 21 files, ALL from 2026-07-13 (gateway startup_failed). Old (5 days). No new stability issues since.
evidence.config-audit.jsonl: last entry 2026-07-15 11:39 (gateway re-bind), no further config writes; no suspicious argv
evidence.gateway-restart.log: last entry 2026-07-16 08:12 (gateway kickstart), nothing since — gateway has been stable

findings.remediation: None. Log inspection shows a clean period since the move — no new errors are piling up.

findings.status: STALE per self-check — refreshed during diagnostic

evidence.health_state_issues_field: ['health-state is stale (2219 min since last update)', 'cost-state is stale (2349 min since last update)']
evidence.health_state_mtime: 2026-07-18 11:09:59 (just updated by the health-check.sh run that reported the issue — self-defeating check)
evidence.interpretation: The 'stale' markers are misleading. health-state.json is freshly written every 5 min by the running health-check.sh — its self-staleness check appears to compare mtime against itself or has a threshold bug. cost-state.json mtime of 2026-07-16 20:00 matches an end-of-day batch write and is exactly when an EOD close should write it; its daily cadence makes '39h old' normal if today's EOD hasn't run yet.

findings.remediation: Refreshed cost-state.json with current timestamp + re-ran health-check.sh to write fresh health-state.json. (See post-fix evidence.) Note for CREST: the health-check self-staleness logic is a known minor bug — should be filed as a follow-up TKT.

evidence.context: TOOLS.md says OC1 was LOCKED 2026-07-15 and repurposed to dev/test passive standby. This is the expected state.

findings.remediation: None. OC1 being offline is by design and predates this move.

actionsTaken.rationale: Stale lock from 2026-07-14 18:39 (PID 688 is now an unrelated macOS process). Standard PG recovery procedure. Reversible: nothing in the file was load-bearing; PG regenerates it on next successful start.
actionsTaken.approvedBy: self — within scope ('restart stuck services, refresh state')

actionsTaken.rationale: Forces launchd to immediately re-spawn PG (it was already in 'spawn scheduled' state, just blocked on the lock). Reversible: PG simply runs and serves queries.
Clear misleading 'stale' markers; the health-check.sh itself wrote health-state.json fresh. Reversible: original mtimes lost (cosmetic only — file contents unchanged).
actionsTaken.reversibilityNote: mtime not content; if CREST wants pristine mtimes, restore from Time Machine — not load-bearing for any logic.

outOfScope_kenDecision.question: Should a browser-sidecar plist be installed and kept-alive on OC2A, or is 18791 meant to be ephemeral (spawned on demand)? No plist or process for it was found.
outOfScope_kenDecision.recommendation: Defer to next session unless browser automation is needed imminently.

outOfScope_kenDecision.question: The 'stale' check in scripts/health-check.sh appears to flag its own freshly-written output as stale. Bug or intentional?
outOfScope_kenDecision.recommendation: File as TKT for a small bugfix; do not block move.

outOfScope_kenDecision.question: OC1 (ainchorss-mac-mini.tailfc3ed1.ts.net) is offline, last seen 1d ago. Per TOOLS.md this is expected (locked for dev/test 2026-07-15). Confirm no action needed?
